meta.title: Eva-CLI | Multi-Agent Runtime
meta.description: Eva-CLI is an open-source V1.5.1 CLI runtime for controlled multi-Agent workflows, combining Rust runtime boundaries, Lua capability layers, Topic EventBus routing, memory and knowledge context, adapters, hardware plans, release gates, and GHCR package delivery.

home.heroTagline: A controlled runtime for multi-Agent automation
home.heroLead: Eva-CLI uses Rust for reliable runtime boundaries and Lua for hot-reloadable Agent behavior, then connects external Agents, MCP servers, skills, memory, knowledge bases, and hardware through a unified Topic EventBus.

runtimeSteps.label: Rust Core
runtimeSteps.description: Owns policy, memory, recovery, and process boundaries.

runtimeSteps.label: Lua Agents
runtimeSteps.description: Hot-reloads behavior while keeping capability access explicit.

runtimeSteps.label: Adapters
runtimeSteps.description: Routes CLI, MCP, HTTP, skills, and hardware through permissions.

home.runtimePanelNote: V1.5.1 keeps release checks executable and adds GHCR package delivery behind the same controlled release path.
home.progressTitle: From architecture scaffold to V1.5.1 release and GHCR delivery
home.progressCurrentTitle: V1.5.1 release hardening and GHCR package delivery are implemented
home.progressCurrentBody: The repository now has a compileable Rust workspace, executable CLI commands through release check/security/perf/migration, configuration validation, an in-memory basic loop, task diagnostics, controlled external capability diagnostics, memory context, hardware binding plans, backup and lifecycle checks, CI gates, release documentation, an SVG-illustrated source user manual, and a GitHub Packages container channel at ghcr.io/yetmos/eva-cli:1.5.1 with Buildx provenance and SBOM evidence.
progressNextItems: Add signed installers, OS package-manager packages, and provenance bundles after the GHCR channel remains stable.
Move destructive restore, release pointer, and supervisor activation paths behind explicit apply gates.
Replace in-memory provider/runtime diagnostics with durable stores and real provider process management, then expand the manual alongside the real apply paths.
home.featuredBlogTitle: Latest design notes from the blog
home.featuredBlogBody: Follow the implementation thinking behind Eva-CLI: runtime boundaries, LuaAgent tooling, product direction, and controlled automation design.

progressStages.label: Architecture, module partitioning, and configuration
progressStages.description: System boundaries, runtime model, adapters, memory, discovery, hardware, recovery, website, docs, configuration, and module partitioning are in place.

progressStages.label: Runnable V1.0 runtime and task diagnostics
progressStages.description: The in-memory basic loop, CLI quickstart, local task status/log/cancel diagnostics, and core CI checks are implemented.

progressStages.label: V1.1 to V1.5.1 controlled capability, release gates, and GHCR delivery
progressStages.description: External capability diagnostics, memory context, hardware plans, backup/lifecycle checks, release hardening commands, the source user manual, and GitHub Packages container delivery are implemented.

progressStages.label: Signed installers, provenance, and real apply paths
progressStages.description: Future releases add provider process execution, durable stores, destructive apply gates, signed installers, OS packages, and provenance bundles.

feedback.title: Feedback and suggestions
feedback.body: Send documentation, architecture, release-gate, compatibility, package, or use-case feedback by email. Eva-CLI is at the V1.5.1 release checkpoint with GHCR package delivery, so concrete reports about command output, docs, package pulls, migration gaps, and future apply-path contracts are especially useful.
feedback.subjectPlaceholder: For example: add a quick start example
feedback.messagePlaceholder: Describe the suggestion, problem, or missing content.
feedback.note: Submitting opens your local email client and sends the message to iyetmos@gmail.com.
